# R1.01 · Cache-aside (lazy loading)

> The default caching strategy: the application owns the cache. On a read miss it fetches from the source and fills
> both layers with an expiry; on a write it invalidates the key. Grounded in `EchoCache.Table.fetch/3`
> (`:hit | :l2 | :fill`) over the `ecc:{table}:id` keyspace (`echo/apps/echo_cache`); real Valkey GET / SET PX / DEL.
> Served at /redis-patterns/caching/cache-aside.

## Pages

- [Hub](/redis-patterns/caching/cache-aside): the pattern and its three dives.
- [GET / SET PX miss-fill](/redis-patterns/caching/cache-aside/miss-fill): `Table.fetch` checks L1, then L2; on a miss `launch_flight` reads the source and writes both layers with `SET … PX` so the next read is a hit.
- [Explicit invalidation](/redis-patterns/caching/cache-aside/invalidation): `Table.invalidate` drops both layers with DEL, and the version-safe `coherence_drop` Lua that never erases a newer row.
- [TTL & staleness](/redis-patterns/caching/cache-aside/ttl-staleness): the jittered `expires_at` TTL as a backstop under a missed invalidation, and the bounded staleness window.

## Up

- [R1 · Caching](/redis-patterns/caching) · [Course home](/redis-patterns)
